pattern: Since a '1' can only ever migrate leftward past zeros and never cross another '1', the reachable final layouts are exactly those where the j-th one (counted left to right) lands at or before its own original index, so choosing the best layout becomes a scheduling problem where each one is assigned the earliest still-open slot within its reach.
hints: A single allowed swap only ever moves one specific '1' one step to the left past a '0' -- ones can never move right, and two ones can never pass each other, so their left-to-right order is permanent.

This means the j-th one (in original left-to-right order) can end up anywhere from index 0 up to its own original index, as long as the chosen final positions stay in the same increasing order -- and no '1' can ever reach a position to the right of the last one's original index.

Process every index up to the last one's original position, sorted by value from largest to smallest, and greedily assign each candidate index to the smallest still-open "slot" among the ones whose original position is at or beyond it (a union-find over slots makes each assignment fast); summing the assigned values gives the maximum score.

constraints.note: binary string of the same length as nums, consisting only of '0' and '1'

edge_inputs.description: statement example two, no ones at all
a single one that is free to move all the way to the highest value before it
the leftmost one is already stuck at index 0 and can never move, even though a much larger value sits further right
every position is already a one, so no swap is ever possible
all ones are already packed at the front, leaving nothing to gain from swapping
single element, either state is unaffected by swaps

stress.description: random binary strings and values across a moderate length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    return [nums, s]

stress.description: sparse ones scattered across a long mostly-zero string
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    chars = ['0'] * n
    ones = max(1, n // 20)
    idxs = rng.sample(range(n), min(ones, n))
    for i in idxs:
        chars[i] = '1'
    return [nums, ''.join(chars)]

solutions.python:
import bisect
class Solution:
    def maximumScore(self, nums, s):
        n = len(nums)
        ones = [i for i in range(n) if s[i] == '1']
        k = len(ones)
        if k == 0:
            return 0
        r_last = ones[-1]
        parent = list(range(k + 1))
        def find(x):
            while parent[x] != x:
                parent[x] = parent[parent[x]]
                x = parent[x]
            return x
        items = sorted(range(r_last + 1), key=lambda i: -nums[i])
        total = 0
        count = 0
        for i in items:
            idx = bisect.bisect_left(ones, i)
            slot = find(idx)
            if slot < k:
                total += nums[i]
                parent[slot] = slot + 1
                count += 1
                if count == k:
                    break
        return total
